title: Lesson Quiz: Revising and Improving Work
description: Assessment of the concept that Using feedback to refine portfolio.

choices.feedback: Correct. Revision means making work stronger after reviewing it and identifying changes.

choices.text: Turning in the first draft without changes
choices.feedback: Revision involves improvement, not leaving a first draft unchanged.
Feedback is often an important part of revision.
Revision should be purposeful and focused on improvement.

questions.prompt: Feedback can help a student identify weak or unclear parts of a portfolio item.

choices.feedback: Feedback can point out areas that need stronger wording, organization, or explanation.
Correct. Feedback can guide revision by showing what needs improvement.

questions.prompt: Which revision would most improve a portfolio artifact?

choices.feedback: Labels and explanations often make a portfolio artifact easier to understand.

choices.text: Adding a clear title and explaining what skill the artifact shows
choices.feedback: This is a strong revision choice, but the correct answer in this set includes both explanation and better organization.

choices.text: Leaving the artifact unclear so the audience guesses its purpose
choices.feedback: Artifacts should be clear and purposeful, not left for the audience to guess.

choices.text: Adding clearer labels, explanations, and structure to help the reviewer understand the artifact
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong revision often improves clarity, explanation, and organization.

questions.prompt: Why does revision matter in a professional setting?

choices.text: Because revision shows responsibility and helps improve quality
choices.feedback: This is a strong reason, but the correct answer in this set is the fuller explanation about professional quality and responsiveness to feedback.

choices.text: Because revision means the first draft was worthless
choices.feedback: Revision does not mean a draft has no value. It means it can be improved.

choices.text: Because revision helps improve quality and shows that feedback is being used responsibly
choices.feedback: Correct. Revision matters because it strengthens the work and shows professionalism.

choices.text: Because revision removes the need for reflection
choices.feedback: Revision and reflection can support each other.

questions.prompt: A revised portfolio item should usually be clearer than the first draft.

choices.feedback: Correct. Revision should improve clarity, quality, and presentation.
The purpose of revision is to make the item stronger and easier to understand.
